compliant_and_na_checks: 2.1.6 — FTP server daemons not installed or active (PASS)

tier_1.title: sshd_config file permissions (600, root:root)
tier_1.vulnerability: World-readable SSH config exposes cipher suite selection and server key paths to any local user.

tier_1.title: PermitRootLogin no
tier_1.vulnerability: Direct root SSH login means a brute-forced credential gives immediate full control with no audit trail.

tier_1.title: MaxAuthTries <= 4
tier_1.vulnerability: Without this, an attacker can attempt unlimited password guesses per connection before disconnecting.

tier_1.title: SSH idle timeout (ClientAliveInterval <= 15, CountMax <= 3)
tier_1.vulnerability: An unattended idle SSH session left open indefinitely is an open door for anyone with physical access.

tier_1.title: LoginGraceTime <= 60 seconds
tier_1.vulnerability: Limits the authentication window — prevents slow brute-force and connection slot exhaustion.

tier_1.title: telnet package not installed
tier_1.vulnerability: Telnet transmits username, password, and all session data in plaintext — trivially captured on any shared network.

tier_1.title: ftp client package not installed
tier_1.vulnerability: FTP sends credentials in cleartext; use sftp or scp instead.

tier_1.title: ufw installed and active
tier_1.message: ufw is installed but not active (Status: inactive).
tier_1.vulnerability: Without a firewall, every service listening on any port is reachable from the local network by default.

tier_1.title: ufw default incoming policy is DENY
tier_1.message: ufw is not active; cannot evaluate default policy.
tier_1.vulnerability: Fail-closed firewall stance requires ufw to be active with incoming denied by default.

tier_1.title: ufw default routed/forwarded policy is DENY
tier_1.message: ufw is not active; cannot evaluate forward policy.
tier_1.vulnerability: A personal laptop must not forward traffic between its interfaces — that is router behaviour.

tier_1.title: /etc/crontab permissions 0600, root:root
tier_1.vulnerability: A world-readable or world-writable crontab lets any user read scheduled commands or inject their own.

tier_1.title: /etc/cron.d/ directory 0700 root:root; files 0600
tier_1.message: Directory mode is 0o755 (expected 0700); Files with bad permissions: e2scrub_all (mode=0o644)
tier_1.vulnerability: Drop-in cron job files in /etc/cron.d/ must be root-exclusive; other users must not add scheduled tasks here.

tier_3.title: AllowTcpForwarding no and X11Forwarding no
tier_3.vulnerability: TCP forwarding tunnels arbitrary traffic through SSH; X11 forwarding exposes your display server remotely.

tier_3.title: SSH MACs — no MD5-based or SHA1-based MACs
tier_3.message: Weak MACs present: umac-64-etm@openssh.com, hmac-sha1-etm@openssh.com, umac-64@openssh.com, hmac-sha1.
tier_3.vulnerability: HMAC-MD5 and HMAC-SHA1 are deprecated; SHA1 collision attacks make message forgery possible.
